headline: Does AbbVie’s Durham AI Plant and Ovarian Cancer Data Reframe the Bull Case for ABBV?
summary: AbbVie recently announced it will invest US$1.40 billion to build a 185-acre advanced pharmaceutical manufacturing campus in Durham, North Carolina, integrating AI-enabled production for its immunology, neuroscience and oncology medicines and creating 734 permanent roles and more than 2,000 construction jobs, with completion targeted by the end of 2028. At the same time, late-breaking Phase 2 data for AbbVie’s antibody-drug conjugate mirvetuximab soravtansine-gynx (ELAHERE) in...
